calendar.matters:
PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-24-2022 Granted Per Written Order

PETITION FOR SPOUSAL PROPERTY
Off Calendar Aug-10-2022 Continued To Aug-24-22 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Bruce Bernstein, in person, for and with Yangping He, Petitioner.

The court advises Mr. Bernstein of its tentative ruling that the argument presented to the court about Family Code and Evidence Code 662 is wrong and that there is no evidence of an inter-spousal transaction. Counsel offers history of the decedent and his client's relationship in China and subsequent move to the United States. Counsel reports of how the decedent gathered funds to purchase the home and that it was purchased prior to the decedent's wife and children immigrating to the United States from China. Ms. He provides additional information regarding the decedent's history as well as her personal history with the decedent. Counsel advises of disclaimers signed by his children. The court advises counsel that it will need additional evidence in support his petition, formally filed before it will be considered.

After additional discussion, the matter is continued to Aug-24-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204. The court advises that if it is unable to pre-grant the petition prior to the next calling of this matter, the court intends to continue the hearing to Sep-07-2022 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Hon. Joseph Quinn. Clerk: Robert Woods. Deputy: Singh. Not Reported.
